title: AtlasStudio Provider Registry Entry
description: Machine-checkable definition of one entry in the Provider Registry described by atlas-core/providers/README.md ('Each provider should eventually have a JSON definition with...'). This schema formalizes that registry direction; it does not itself populate atlas-core/providers/, which is outside this document's allowed change paths. A future work order should add the actual per-provider JSON files there, validated against this schema. Volatile session/quota status is deliberately not part of this schema - see schemas/agent-status.schema.json, which changes far more often than a provider's identity and capabilities.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier, e.g. gpt, claude-code, codex, github-copilot, ollama, human, matching studio/work-order-format.md's Recommended Agents list and schemas/work-order.schema.json's recommended_agent/fallback_agent enums.

provider_type.description: Broad kind of provider. `human` is reserved for the id human, per 'Human As Provider' in studio/orchestration/capability-based-orchestration.md.

locality.description: Where this provider runs. `n/a` applies only to provider_type human.

capability_id.description: id of a schemas/capability.schema.json entry this provider can supply.

confidence.description: 0-3 scale: 3 primary strength (prefer this provider), 2 competent/acceptable, 1 possible but wasteful or needs review, 0 avoid. Same scale studio/scheduling/agent-scheduler-design.md used for named agents; here it is scoped per capability_id instead of per fixed task class, per ADR-0007's capability-first replacement.

capabilities_supplied.description: Every capability this provider can supply, each with its own confidence score. A provider with no entry for a capability is treated as confidence 0 for it.

risk_ceiling.description: Highest risk_level this provider may take unreviewed, per studio/agent-assignment-model.md's Risk-Based Routing. Work above this ceiling requires a REVIEWED_BY provider.human condition.

context_depth.description: How much cross-document or cross-graph context this provider can hold reliably in one session. Work orders spanning many documents need medium or deep.

scarcity.description: Quota-conservation signal, not a capability rating: how precious this provider's available sessions are. Used to prefer cheap/local providers for low-stakes work so scarce strong providers stay available for work that needs them.

cost_class.description: Free-text relative cost signal, e.g. 'free', 'subscription', 'metered-high'. Not a source of truth for billing.

strengths.description: Free-text summary of what this provider is best used for, restating studio/agent-roles.md as short labels rather than paraphrasing its prose.

avoid_when.description: Free-text conditions under which this provider should not be assigned even if capability confidence is nonzero, restating studio/agent-roles.md's 'Avoid assigning' guidance.
